seoTitle: Point Vernon vs Pony Hills — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Vernon and Pony Hills. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Vernon vs Pony Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Vernon and Pony Hills.
heading: Point Vernon vs Pony Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Vernon, Queensland and Pony Hills, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pony Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Point Vernon (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Vernon or Pony Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Vernon has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Pony Hills scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Vernon scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Pony Hills scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Vernon or Pony Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pony Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
